title: capparis roxburghii
intro: Capparis roxburghii, also known as the Himalayan caper or Roxburgh's capper, is a flowering plant species of the genus Capparis, native to the Himalayas and Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Capparis roxburghii include reducing fever, treating wounds and skin conditions, and providing relief from digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Capparis roxburghii include infusions, decoctions, and powder forms, which can be used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
